content.text:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the mindmap implementation, I've identified the three root causes:

## Root Causes Analysis

### 1. Central Node Shows "undefined" (Title Issue)
**File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/markmap-renderer.tsx` **Lines:** 33-53, 47

**Root Cause:** When the `MindmapRenderer` converts nodes to markdown via the `nodesToMarkdown()` function, it uses the `title` prop directly (line 47: `# ${title}`). If the title is undefined when loaded from the database, the markdown renders as `# undefined`. 

The issue occurs because:
- Voice tool calls `create_mindmap` with both `title` and `topic` fields
- Mindmap handler (line 90 in `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/mindmap-handler.ts`): `topic: title` - stores as `topic` in data
- When saved via `saveMaterialToAPI` (use-saved-materials.ts:247), the title from mindmap object is passed
- BUT if the AI doesn't provide a proper title or passes undefined, it won't be caught by validation

**The validation happens AFTER the mindmap is created** (mindmap-handler.ts:68-74), which only validates the handler received a title, not what gets saved.

---

### 2. Flat Structure - All Nodes at Same Level
**Files:** 
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/markmap-renderer.tsx` **Lines:** 33-53
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/mindmap-handler.ts` **Lines:** 14-56

**Root Cause:** **Critical mismatch between data structures:**

- **mindmap-handler.ts** generates nodes with `parentId` property to establish hierarchy (lines 19-47, 91-95)
- **markmap-renderer.tsx** `nodesToMarkdown()` expects nodes with `children` property (line 38: `node.children`)

When nodes from the AI have structure like:
```json
{ "id": "1", "label": "Topic", "parentId": null }
{ "id": "2", "label": "Subtopic", "parentId": "1" }
```

But `nodesToMarkdown()` looks for:
```javascript
if (node.children && node.children.length > 0) { ... }
```

Since loaded nodes DON'T have a `children` property, the function can't find the hierarchy and renders everything at the same markdown level (##).

**The fix requires converting parentId-based relationships into a children-based tree structure before rendering.**

---

### 3. Coach Responds "Mi dispiace, non ho capito" When User Says "geografia"
**Files:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/intent-detection.ts` **Lines:** 98-101 (detection works correctly)
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/character-router.ts` **Lines:** 83-89 (routing defined)
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/handoff-manager.ts` **Lines:** 112-120 (handoff intent map)

**Root Cause:** **Intent detection works, but routing logic has a gap:**

1. When user says "geografia", intent-detection.ts correctly detects it as subject=`geography`, type=`academic_help` (line 99)
2. character-router.ts correctly maps geography→'humboldt-geografia' (line 89)
3. **BUT** the handoff intent map (handoff-manager.ts:112-120) shows:
   ```typescript
   academic_help: 'maestro',  // Should route to maestro
   ```

4. **The problem:** Just saying "geografia" doesn't trigger a tool-request intent. The system detects it as `academic_help` intent, which maps to 'maestro' character. However:
   - If no maestro session is active, the system can't handoff immediately
   - The coach is the default fallback character
   - The coach doesn't have geography teaching expertise, so responds with "Mi dispiace, non ho capito"

**The root cause is:** When a user mentions a subject but doesn't explicitly ask for a tool or maestro interaction, the system should either:
- Automatically initiate a maestro session with that subject, OR
- Show the tool-maestro-selection-dialog to let them pick a maestro

Currently it fails to do either, falling back to the coach.

---

## Summary Table

| Issue | File:Line | Root Cause | Impact |
|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| **Undefined Title** | markmap-renderer.tsx:47 | Title not validated at save time; undefined values render as "# undefined" | Central node shows "undefined" |
| **Flat Structure** | markmap-renderer.tsx:38 vs mindmap-handler.ts:91 | Mismatch: handler creates `parentId` nodes, renderer expects `children` property | All nodes appear at same level |
| **Coach Misunderstands "geografia"** | handoff-manager.ts:113, character-router.ts:89 | No automatic maestro routing for detected subjects; falls back to coach | Coach responds "non ho capito" |

content.thinking:
Ora ho visto abbastanza per fare una diagnosi completa. Riassumo i problemi identificati:

## PROBLEMA 1: Central Node "undefined"

Guardando il flusso:
1. `voice-tool-commands.ts:1108` - l'API chiama con `title: args.title || args.name || 'Untitled'`
2. Il tool definition richiede sia `title` che `topic`
3. Ma nel handler `mindmap-handler.ts:90` fa `topic: title` - usa il title

Il problema sembra essere che:
- La UI (MindmapRenderer) riceve i dati dal database via `useMindmaps`
- Quando viene salvato al database, il title potrebbe essere passato male
- Devo vedere come viene passato il title da API a database

Ma più probabilmente il problema è come i dati arrivano al renderer. Guardo `use-saved-materials.ts` per vedere come vengono caricati i mindmaps...

Aspetta, vedo che nello screenshot il nodo centrale è "undefined". Questo significa che quando viene passato il markdown al renderer, `title` è undefined.

Guardando `markmap-renderer.tsx:47`: `let markdown = \`# ${title}\n\`;`
Se title è undefined, il markdown sarà `# undefined`.

Ma dove arriva il title? Da `mindmaps-view.tsx:547`:
```tsx
<MindmapRenderer
  title={selectedMindmap.title}
  nodes={selectedMindmap.nodes}
/>
```

Quindi `selectedMindmap.title` è undefined. Da dove viene `selectedMindmap`? Dal database via `useMindmaps`.

Devo vedere come i dati vengono salvati e caricati... Ma questo è un problema secondario. Il problema principale è che l'AI non genera la mappa con struttura corretta.


Nell'implementazione attuale, la generazione dei nodi presenta una struttura piatta senza gerarchia. Il sistema richiede una struttura con `parentId` per definire correttamente le relazioni tra i nodi, ma l'AI non sta rispettando questo requisito.

I principali punti critici sono:
1. Manca la gerarchia nei nodi generati
2. Non viene utilizzato il campo `parentId`
3. La struttura risultante è lineare invece che gerarchica

Serve un intervento per modificare la logica di generazione dei nodi dell'AI. I see a critical structural mismatch between data storage and rendering. The database stores nodes with `parentId`, while the renderer expects a nested `children` structure. This means an intermediate transformation is necessary to convert flat `parentId`-based data into a hierarchical tree before visualization. The current implementation lacks this crucial conversion step, which explains why all nodes appear at the same visual level despite having potential parent-child relationships defined.

The renderer's markdown generation assumes a pre-existing tree structure, but receives a flat array. This suggests we need to implement a preprocessing function that reconstructs the hierarchical relationships by grouping nodes based on their `parentId` before passing them to the rendering component.

The interaction reveals a communication breakdown where the AI coach fails to properly interpret a single-word input. The system appears to lack robust context handling, causing a generic "I didn't understand" response when presented with a specific topic like "geografia".

The root cause likely stems from incomplete context transmission or tool call implementation issues. When the user specifies geography after being prompted about themes, the AI should generate a map focused on that region, but instead produces a generic error message.

Potential technical gaps include incomplete context routing, tool call failures, or content generation problems that trigger fallback error handling in the frontend interface.

The key debugging points center on ensuring proper data mapping, content generation, and error state management across the AI interaction flow.

content.text:
Ora ho identificato tutti i problemi. Ecco l'analisi completa:

---

## Analisi dei Problemi - Mappa Mentale "La Liguria"

### PROBLEMA 1: Nodo centrale "undefined"

**Root cause**: Mismatch tra come i dati vengono salvati e caricati.

- `mindmap-handler.ts:90` salva i dati come `{ topic: title, nodes, markdown }`
- Quando vengono caricati dal database, il campo si chiama `topic`
- Ma `mindmaps-view.tsx:547` passa a `MindmapRenderer`:
  ```tsx
  <MindmapRenderer title={selectedMindmap.title} nodes={selectedMindmap.nodes} />
  ```
- Se nel database il campo è `topic` invece di `title`, il renderer riceve `title=undefined`

---

### PROBLEMA 2: Struttura piatta (no gerarchia)

**Root cause**: **MISMATCH CRITICO tra formati dati**

1. **mindmap-handler.ts:91-95** salva nodi con `parentId`:
   ```ts
   nodes: nodes.map((n) => ({
     id: n.id,
     label: n.label,
     parentId: n.parentId || null,  // <-- parentId!
   })),
   ```

2. **markmap-renderer.tsx:33-53** `nodesToMarkdown()` si aspetta nodi con `children`:
   ```ts
   if (node.children && node.children.length > 0) {  // <-- children!
     for (const child of node.children) { ... }
   }
   ```

3. **mindmaps-view.tsx:547** NON passa il `markdown` pre-generato:
   ```tsx
   <MindmapRenderer title={...} nodes={...} />  // manca markdown!
   ```

**Risultato**: Il renderer riceve nodi con `parentId`, non trova `children`, e renderizza tutto allo stesso livello.

Il handler genera correttamente il markdown gerarchico (`mindmap-handler.ts:87`), ma non viene mai usato!

---

### PROBLEMA 3: Coach dice "Mi dispiace, non ho capito"

**Root cause**: Quando l'AI fa un tool call, potrebbe non generare testo di risposta.

- `character-chat-view.tsx:347`:
  ```ts
  content: data.content || data.message || 'Mi dispiace, non ho capito.'
  ```

Se l'AI risponde solo con tool_calls e nessun `content`, il frontend mostra il fallback.

**Scenario nello screenshot**: 
1. Melissa chiede "Quali temi vuoi includere?"
2. Utente risponde "geografia"
3. L'AI probabilmente chiama `create_mindmap` ma non genera testo di conferma
4. Frontend mostra "Mi dispiace, non ho capito"

---

### PROBLEMA 4: Mancanza di istruzioni per struttura gerarchica

**Root cause**: Il tool definition (`voice-tool-commands.ts:276-311`) non spiega all'AI COME strutturare i nodi.

```ts
nodes: {
  type: 'array',
  description: 'Nodi della mappa (opzionale, possono essere generati)',  // <-- vago!
  items: {
    type: 'object',
    properties: {
      id: { type: 'string' },
      label: { type: 'string' },
      parentId: { type: 'string' },  // <-- non spiega come usarlo
    },
  },
},
```

E in `TOOL_USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS` (line 1244) dice solo "struttura chiara e gerarchica" ma non mostra un esempio concreto di come usare `parentId` per creare gerarchia.

---

## Riepilogo Bug

| # | Problema | File | Causa |
|---|----------|------|-------|
| 1 | Central node "undefined" | mindmaps-view.tsx, use-saved-materials.ts | Campo `topic` vs `title` mismatch |
| 2 | Struttura piatta | markmap-renderer.tsx:38 | Aspetta `children`, riceve `parentId` |
| 3 | "Non ho capito" | character-chat-view.tsx:347 | AI fa tool call senza generare content |
| 4 | Nodi non gerarchici | voice-tool-commands.ts:296-306 | Mancano istruzioni/esempi per AI |

Vuoi che prepari le fix?

file.content:
# ADR 0011: Voice Commands for Mindmap Modifications

## Status
Accepted

## Date
2025-12-31

## Context

ConvergioEdu implements a "conversation-first" approach where students interact with AI Maestri through natural dialogue rather than forms. Issue #23 established this principle, and Issue #44 extended it to mindmaps with real-time voice commands.

### Problem

When students build mindmaps with Maestri, they should be able to modify the map through voice:
- "Aggiungi un nodo su Roma" (Add a node about Rome)
- "Collega geografia con storia" (Connect geography to history)
- "Espandi il nodo sulla Liguria" (Expand the Liguria node)
- "Cancella il nodo sbagliato" (Delete the wrong node)

The existing SSE infrastructure (ADR-0005) supports real-time events, but there was no:
1. Hook for components to receive modification events
2. Imperative API for the mindmap renderer to apply modifications
3. Wrapper component combining these capabilities

### Options Considered

#### Option 1: State-based Updates
Pass modification commands as props to the renderer.

**Cons:**
- React re-render on every modification
- Complex state reconciliation
- No animation control

#### Option 2: Imperative Handle API (Chosen)
Expose methods via `useImperativeHandle` for direct DOM manipulation.

**Pros:**
- Direct control over markmap-view library
- Smooth animations with D3
- No unnecessary re-renders
- Consistent with React patterns for library integration

#### Option 3: Custom Events
Use DOM CustomEvents for communication.

**Cons:**
- Non-React pattern
- Harder to type-check
- Global event pollution

## Decision

Implement a three-layer architecture:

### 1. SSE Hook: `useMindmapModifications`

```typescript
// src/lib/hooks/use-mindmap-modifications.ts
export function useMindmapModifications({
  sessionId,
  enabled,
  callbacks,
}: UseMindmapModificationsOptions): UseMindmapModificationsResult {
  // Subscribe to /api/tools/sse?sessionId=X
  // Dispatch to callbacks on mindmap:modify events
}
```

Listens for SSE events of type `mindmap:modify` and routes to appropriate callbacks.

### 2. Interactive Renderer: `InteractiveMarkMapRenderer`

```typescript
// src/components/tools/interactive-markmap-renderer.tsx
export interface InteractiveMarkMapHandle {
  addNode: (concept: string, parentNode?: string) => void;
  expandNode: (node: string, suggestions?: string[]) => void;
  deleteNode: (node: string) => void;
  focusNode: (node: string) => void;
  setNodeColor: (node: string, color: string) => void;
  connectNodes: (nodeA: string, nodeB: string) => void;
  getNodes: () => MindmapNode[];
  undo: () => void;
}
```

Extends the base MarkMapRenderer with:
- Imperative modification methods
- Node state management
- Undo history
- D3 animations for focus/highlight

### 3. Wrapper Component: `LiveMindmap`

```typescript
// src/components/tools/live-mindmap.tsx
export function LiveMindmap({
  sessionId,
  listenForEvents = true,
  onNodesChange,
  onModification,
  ...rendererProps
}: LiveMindmapProps) {
  // Combines InteractiveMarkMapRenderer with useMindmapModifications
}
```

## Voice Command Flow

```
Student: "Aggiungi Roma come sottocategoria di Italia"
                    │
                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               Azure OpenAI Realtime API                      │
│  Transcribes and interprets voice command                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         POST /api/tools/stream/modify                        │
│  { command: 'mindmap_add_node',                             │
│    args: { concept: 'Roma', parentNode: 'Italia' } }        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         SSE Broadcast (tool-events.ts)                       │
│  Event: 'mindmap:modify'                                     │
│  Data: { command, args, sessionId, maestroId }              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         useMindmapModifications Hook                         │
│  Receives event, calls callbacks.onAddNode('Roma', 'Italia')│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         InteractiveMarkMapRenderer                           │
│  rendererRef.current.addNode('Roma', 'Italia')              │
│  - Finds parent node 'Italia' by fuzzy match               │
│  - Creates new child node                                    │
│  - Re-renders with D3 transition                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Supported Commands

| Voice Command | Function | Implementation |
|--------------|----------|----------------|
| `mindmap_add_node` | Add concept as child node | `addNode(concept, parentNode?)` |
| `mindmap_connect_nodes` | Create link between nodes | `connectNodes(nodeA, nodeB)` |
| `mindmap_expand_node` | Add multiple children | `expandNode(node, suggestions?)` |
| `mindmap_delete_node` | Remove node and children | `deleteNode(node)` |
| `mindmap_focus_node` | Center view on node | `focusNode(node)` |
| `mindmap_set_color` | Change node color | `setNodeColor(node, color)` |

## Node Matching

Commands reference nodes by label text. The renderer uses fuzzy matching:

```typescript
private findNodeByLabel(label: string): SVGGElement | null {
  const normalizedLabel = label.toLowerCase().trim();
  // First try exact match, then includes, then Levenshtein
}
```

## Consequences

### Positive
- Natural voice interaction for mindmap building
- Real-time updates visible to all session participants
- Smooth D3 animations for professional feel
- Undo support for mistake recovery
- Extensible to other tool types

### Negative
- Fuzzy matching may select wrong node
- No conflict resolution for multi-user edits (see Phase 8)
- Voice transcription errors can cause wrong commands

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/lib/hooks/use-mindmap-modifications.ts` | SSE subscription hook |
| `src/components/tools/interactive-markmap-renderer.tsx` | Imperative renderer |
| `src/components/tools/live-mindmap.tsx` | Combined component |
| `src/lib/voice/voice-tool-commands.ts` | Voice command definitions |
| `src/app/api/tools/stream/modify/route.ts` | Modification API endpoint |

## References
- ADR 0005: Real-time SSE Architecture
- ADR 0009: Tool Execution Architecture
- Issue #23: Conversation-First Architecture
- Issue #44: Phase 7-9 Mindmap Features
- ManifestoEdu.md: "Forms are a sign we failed"

1→# MaestroToolsPlanDec30 - Interactive Tools & Materials System
     2→
     3→**Data**: 2025-12-30
     4→**Target**: Complete tool execution + Materials archive + UI improvements
     5→**Metodo**: VERIFICA BRUTALE - ogni task testato prima di dichiararlo fatto
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 🎯 ESEGUI PIANO - UN SOLO COMANDO
    10→
    11→**Apri Kitty, poi copia e incolla QUESTO:**
    12→
    13→```
    14→Esegui il piano in /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md - Sei il COORDINATORE (CLAUDE 1). Workflow: 1) Crea branch e worktree per ogni Claude (vedi sezione GIT WORKFLOW), 2) Lancia 4 Claude con claude-parallel.sh, 3) Invia ogni Claude al suo worktree, 4) Invia i task, 5) Monitora e sincronizza i gate, 6) Quando ogni Claude finisce deve fare commit+push+PR, 7) Tu fai merge delle PR in ordine, 8) Cleanup worktree e branch. Segui le regole BRUTAL MODE. Mai chiudere issue senza prova.
    15→```
    16→
    17→**Tutto qui. CLAUDE 1 leggerà il piano e farà il resto.**
    18→
    19→---
    20→
    21→## 🚀 KITTY ORCHESTRATION - DETTAGLI
    22→
    23→> **LEGGI PRIMA DI TUTTO** - Istruzioni per parallelizzare
    24→
    25→### Pre-requisiti
    26→```bash
    27→# DEVE essere eseguito da Kitty (non Warp, non iTerm)
    28→# Verifica di essere in Kitty:
    29→echo $KITTY_PID  # Deve stampare un numero
    30→
    31→# Verifica remote control attivo:
    32→~/.claude/scripts/kitty-check.sh
    33→```
    34→
    35→### GIT WORKFLOW (OBBLIGATORIO)
    36→
    37→**Ogni Claude lavora in un worktree separato. Ogni fase = 1 PR.**
    38→
    39→#### STEP 0: Setup Worktrees (CLAUDE 1 fa questo PRIMA di tutto)
    40→
    41→```bash
    42→cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu
    43→
    44→# Crea branch per ogni fase
    45→git checkout MirrorBuddy
    46→git branch feature/tools-phase1-backend
    47→git branch feature/tools-phase2-ui
    48→git branch feature/tools-phase3-integration
    49→
    50→# Crea worktree per ogni Claude
    51→git worktree add ../ConvergioEdu-C2 feature/tools-phase1-backend
    52→git worktree add ../ConvergioEdu-C3 feature/tools-phase2-ui
    53→git worktree add ../ConvergioEdu-C4 feature/tools-phase3-integration
    54→
    55→# Verifica
    56→git worktree list
    57→```
    58→
    59→#### Mapping Claude → Worktree → Branch
    60→
    61→| Claude | Worktree | Branch | PR |
    62→|--------|----------|--------|-----|
    63→| CLAUDE 1 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu` | MirrorBuddy | Coordina solo |
    64→| CLAUDE 2 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C2` | feature/tools-phase1-backend | PR #1 |
    65→| CLAUDE 3 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C3` | feature/tools-phase2-ui | PR #2 |
    66→| CLAUDE 4 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C4` | feature/tools-phase3-integration | PR #3 |
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→### Launch Sequence (DA ESEGUIRE IN ORDINE)
    71→
    72→**STEP 1: Lancia i Claude worker (dal terminale Kitty)**
    73→```bash
    74→~/.claude/scripts/claude-parallel.sh 4
    75→```
    76→
    77→**STEP 2: Invia ogni Claude al suo worktree**
    78→```bash
    79→kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-2 "cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C2" && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-2 Return
    80→kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-3 "cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C3" && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-3 Return
    81→kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-4 "cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C4" && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-4 Return
    82→```
    83→
    84→**STEP 3: Invia i task**
    85→```bash
    86→kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-2 "Leggi /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md, sei CLAUDE 2 (BACKEND). Lavori nel worktree feature/tools-phase1-backend. Esegui T-00 → T-06. Quando finisci: commit, push, crea PR verso MirrorBuddy." && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-2 Return
    87→
    88→kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-3 "Leggi /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md, sei CLAUDE 3 (FRONTEND). Lavori nel worktree feature/tools-phase2-ui. ATTENDI GATE-1 poi esegui T-07 → T-12. Quando finisci: commit, push, crea PR." && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-3 Return
    89→
    90→kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-4 "Leggi /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md, sei CLAUDE 4 (INTEGRATION). Lavori nel worktree feature/tools-phase3-integration. ATTENDI GATE-2 poi esegui T-15 → T-22. Quando finisci: commit, push, crea PR." && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-4 Return
    91→```
    92→
    93→---
    94→
    95→### PR Workflow (ogni Claude fa questo quando completa la sua fase)
    96→

   213→## OVERVIEW
   214→
   215→### The Problem
   216→1. **#39**: Chat API has NO function calling - Maestros just output text
   217→2. **#36**: No Tool Panel - tools render inline in chat (unusable)
   218→3. **#35, #38**: No tool buttons on maestro cards or conversation
   219→4. **#37**: No Archive - all created content is LOST
   220→5. **#22**: No IndexedDB storage for materials
   221→6. **#19**: Still says "Compiti", no Subject→Maestro flow
   222→7. **#14, #27**: Notifications exist but NEVER triggered
   223→
   224→### The Solution
   225→Complete system with:
   226→- OpenAI function calling in chat API (#39)
   227→- Tool panel with video conference layout (#36)
   228→- Tool buttons everywhere (#35, #38)
   229→- Unified archive for ALL content (#37)
   230→- IndexedDB for materials (#22)
   231→- Materials page redesign (#19)
   232→- Notification triggers (#14)
   233→
   234→---
   235→
   236→## USE CASES
   237→
   238→| Action | Tool | Result |
   239→|--------|------|--------|
   240→| Click "Mappa" | Mind Map | Maestro creates interactive mind map |
   241→| Click "Quiz" | Quiz | Maestro creates quiz, evaluates answers |
   242→| Click "Demo" | Demo | Maestro generates HTML/JS simulation |
   243→| Click "Cerca" | Search | Search web/YouTube for educational content |
   244→| Click "Flashcard" | Flashcard | Maestro creates flashcard deck |
   245→| Click "Webcam" | Upload | Capture photo → detect subject → route to Maestro |
   246→| Upload PDF | Upload | PDF pages → detect subject → route to Maestro |
   247→| Browse Archive | Archive | Filter/sort all created tools and materials |
   248→

   251→## ARCHITECTURE
   252→
   253→### Tool Execution Flow
   254→```
   255→Button click OR Maestro decides
   256→        ↓
   257→Chat API with OpenAI function calling (#39)
   258→        ↓
   259→Tool handler executes
   260→        ↓
   261→SSE broadcasts updates (#26)
   262→        ↓
   263→Tool Panel renders in real-time (#36)
   264→        ↓
   265→Save to Archive (IndexedDB #22 + Prisma)
   266→        ↓
   267→Available in Archive page (#37)
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Video Conference Layout (#36)
   271→```
   272→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   273→│ [Avatar] Maestro Name    [Close Panel]  │  10%
   274→├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
   275→│                                         │
   276→│         TOOL CONTENT PANEL              │  70%
   277→│    (Mind Map / Quiz / Demo / Webcam)    │
   278→│                                         │
   279→├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
   280→│  Chat messages (scrollable)             │  15%
   281→├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
   282→│  [Input] [Mappa][Quiz][Demo][Cam][...]  │  5%
   283→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   284→```
   285→
   286→### Materials Flow (#19)
   287→```
   288→Upload (Webcam/File/PDF)
   289→        ↓
   290→Azure Vision API analyzes
   291→        ↓
   292→Subject detected (math, science, etc.)
   293→        ↓
   294→User confirms subject
   295→        ↓
   296→Route to appropriate Maestro
   297→        ↓
   298→Maieutic guidance begins
   299→        ↓
   300→Save to Materials Archive (#37)
   301→```

   548→## CLAUDE 2: PHASE 0 (Preparation)
   549→
   550→### T-00: ADR - Tool Execution Architecture
   551→
   552→Create `docs/adr/0004-tool-execution-architecture.md`:
   553→
   554→```markdown
   555→# ADR 0004: Tool Execution Architecture
   556→
   557→## Status
   558→Accepted
   559→
   560→## Context
   561→I Maestri AI devono poter creare strumenti interattivi (mind maps, quiz, demo, flashcard)
   562→durante le conversazioni. Attualmente il chat API NON ha function calling - i Maestri
   563→scrivono solo testo come "Usa lo strumento create_mindmap..." senza eseguire nulla.
   564→
   565→Problemi attuali:
   566→1. Chat API non supporta OpenAI function calling
   567→2. Nessun tool panel per visualizzare i tool creati
   568→3. Nessun storage per salvare i tool
   569→4. Nessun archivio per rivedere tool passati
   570→
   571→Opzioni considerate:
   572→1. Client-side parsing del testo → fragile, non affidabile
   573→2. Streaming custom → complesso, non standard
   574→3. OpenAI function calling → standard, supportato, affidabile
   575→
   576→## Decision
   577→Implementiamo OpenAI function calling nel chat API con:
   578→- `tools` parameter con CHAT_TOOL_DEFINITIONS
   579→- Tool executor framework con handler registry
   580→- SSE per aggiornamenti real-time
   581→- IndexedDB per storage materiali
   582→- Prisma CreatedTool model per persistenza
   583→
   584→## Consequences
   585→
   586→### Positive
   587→- Standard OpenAI API, ben documentato
   588→- Affidabile: il modello decide quando chiamare i tool
   589→- Estensibile: nuovi tool = nuovi handler
   590→- Real-time: SSE per progress updates
   591→
   592→### Negative
   593→- Richiede Azure OpenAI (Ollama non supporta function calling)
   594→- Complessità aggiuntiva nel frontend
   595→
   596→## References
   597→- OpenAI Function Calling: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling
   598→- Issue #39: Chat API missing function calling
   599→- Issue #23: Conversation-First Architecture
   600→```

   709→
   710→```markdown
   711→### Tool Execution System
   712→
   713→> **Architecture Decision**: ADR 0004 - Tool Execution Architecture
   714→
   715→The Maestri can create interactive tools during conversations using OpenAI function calling.
   716→
   717→#### Key Files
   718→
   719→| File | Purpose |
   720→|------|---------|
   721→| `src/types/tools.ts` | Tool types and OpenAI function definitions |
   722→| `src/lib/tools/tool-executor.ts` | Handler registry and execution |
   723→| `src/lib/tools/handlers/` | Individual tool handlers |
   724→| `src/components/tools/tool-panel.tsx` | Tool visualization panel |
   725→| `src/components/tools/demo-sandbox.tsx` | Secure iframe for demos |
   726→| `src/lib/storage/materials-db.ts` | IndexedDB for materials |
   727→
   728→#### Tool Types
   729→
   730→\`\`\`typescript
   731→type ToolType = 'mindmap' | 'quiz' | 'flashcard' | 'demo' | 'search' | 'webcam' | 'pdf';
   732→\`\`\`
   733→
   734→#### Tool Flow
   735→
   736→\`\`\`
   737→User clicks tool button OR Maestro decides
   738→        ↓
   739→Chat API with OpenAI function calling
   740→        ↓
   741→Tool executor routes to handler
   742→        ↓
   743→Handler generates content
   744→        ↓
   745→Tool panel renders result
   746→        ↓
   747→Saved to Archive (IndexedDB + Prisma)
   748→\`\`\`
   749→
   750→#### Adding a New Tool
   751→
   752→1. Add type to `ToolType` in `src/types/tools.ts`
   753→2. Add function definition to `CHAT_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`
   754→3. Create handler in `src/lib/tools/handlers/[tool]-handler.ts`
   755→4. Register handler in `src/lib/tools/handlers/index.ts`
   756→5. Add rendering case to `ToolPanel` component
   757→6. Write tests in `__tests__/[tool]-handler.test.ts`
   758→```

   965→### T-03: Tool Executor Framework (#23)
   966→
   967→Create `src/lib/tools/tool-executor.ts`:
   968→
   969→```typescript
   970→import { ToolType, ToolExecutionResult } from '@/types/tools';
   971→import { nanoid } from 'nanoid';
   972→import { broadcastToolEvent } from '@/lib/realtime/tool-events';
   973→
   974→type ToolHandler = (
   975→  args: Record<string, unknown>,
   976→  context: { sessionId?: string; userId?: string; maestroId?: string }
   977→) => Promise<ToolExecutionResult>;
   978→
   979→const handlers = new Map<string, ToolHandler>();
   980→
   981→export function registerToolHandler(functionName: string, handler: ToolHandler) {
   982→  handlers.set(functionName, handler);
   983→}
   984→
   985→export async function executeToolCall(
   986→  functionName: string,
   987→  args: Record<string, unknown>,
   988→  context: { sessionId?: string; userId?: string; maestroId?: string }
   989→): Promise<ToolExecutionResult> {
   990→  const handler = handlers.get(functionName);
   991→
   992→  if (!handler) {
   993→    return {
   994→      success: false,
   995→      toolId: nanoid(),
   996→      toolType: 'mindmap',
   997→      error: `Unknown tool: ${functionName}`,
   998→    };
   999→  }
  1000→
  1001→  const toolId = nanoid();
  1002→
  1003→  // Broadcast start event
  1004→  broadcastToolEvent({
  1005→    type: 'tool_started',
  1006→    toolId,
  1007→    functionName,
  1008→    timestamp: new Date(),
  1009→  });
  1010→
  1011→  try {
  1012→    const result = await handler(args, context);
  1013→
  1014→    // Broadcast complete event
  1015→    broadcastToolEvent({
  1016→      type: 'tool_completed',
  1017→      toolId,
  1018→      data: result.data,
  1019→      timestamp: new Date(),
  1020→    });
  1021→
  1022→    return result;
  1023→  } catch (error) {
  1024→    broadcastToolEvent({
  1025→      type: 'tool_error',
  1026→      toolId,
  1027→      error: String(error),
  1028→      timestamp: new Date(),
  1029→    });
  1030→
  1031→    return {
  1032→      success: false,
  1033→      toolId,
  1034→      toolType: 'mindmap',
  1035→      error: String(error),
  1036→    };
  1037→  }
  1038→}
  1039→```

  1121→
  1122→Create `src/lib/storage/materials-db.ts`:
  1123→
  1124→```typescript
  1125→import { openDB, DBSchema, IDBPDatabase } from 'idb';
  1126→
  1127→interface MaterialsDB extends DBSchema {
  1128→  files: {
  1129→    key: string;
  1130→    value: {
  1131→      id: string;
  1132→      blob: Blob;
  1133→      thumbnail?: Blob;
  1134→      createdAt: number;
  1135→    };
  1136→  };
  1137→  metadata: {
  1138→    key: string;
  1139→    value: {
  1140→      id: string;
  1141→      filename: string;
  1142→      format: 'image' | 'pdf';
  1143→      mimeType: string;
  1144→      subject?: string;
  1145→      maestroId?: string;
  1146→      size: number;
  1147→      pageCount?: number;
  1148→      createdAt: Date;
  1149→      updatedAt: Date;
  1150→    };
  1151→    indexes: {
  1152→      'by-date': Date;
  1153→      'by-subject': string;
  1154→      'by-format': string;
  1155→    };
  1156→  };
  1157→}
  1158→
  1159→let dbInstance: IDBPDatabase<MaterialsDB> | null = null;
  1160→
  1161→export async function getMaterialsDB(): Promise<IDBPDatabase<MaterialsDB>> {
  1162→  if (dbInstance) return dbInstance;
  1163→
  1164→  dbInstance = await openDB<MaterialsDB>('convergio-materials', 1, {
  1165→    upgrade(db) {
  1166→      // Files store (blobs)
  1167→      db.createObjectStore('files', { keyPath: 'id' });
  1168→
  1169→      // Metadata store
  1170→      const metaStore = db.createObjectStore('metadata', { keyPath: 'id' });
  1171→      metaStore.createIndex('by-date', 'createdAt');
  1172→      metaStore.createIndex('by-subject', 'subject');
  1173→      metaStore.createIndex('by-format', 'format');
  1174→    },
  1175→  });
  1176→
  1177→  return dbInstance;
  1178→}
  1179→
  1180→export async function saveMaterial(
  1181→  file: Blob,
  1182→  metadata: Omit<MaterialsDB['metadata']['value'], 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>
  1183→): Promise<string> {
  1184→  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  1185→  const id = crypto.randomUUID();
  1186→  const now = new Date();
  1187→
  1188→  // Generate thumbnail for images
  1189→  let thumbnail: Blob | undefined;
  1190→  if (metadata.format === 'image') {
  1191→    thumbnail = await generateThumbnail(file);
  1192→  }
  1193→
  1194→  await db.put('files', {
  1195→    id,
  1196→    blob: file,
  1197→    thumbnail,
  1198→    createdAt: now.getTime(),
  1199→  });
  1200→
  1201→  await db.put('metadata', {
  1202→    ...metadata,
  1203→    id,
  1204→    createdAt: now,
  1205→    updatedAt: now,
  1206→  });
  1207→
  1208→  return id;
  1209→}
  1210→
  1211→export async function getMaterials(filter?: {
  1212→  subject?: string;
  1213→  format?: 'image' | 'pdf';
  1214→  limit?: number;
  1215→}): Promise<Array<MaterialsDB['metadata']['value']>> {
  1216→  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  1217→  let results = await db.getAll('metadata');
  1218→
  1219→  if (filter?.subject) {
  1220→    results = results.filter(m => m.subject === filter.subject);
  1221→  }
  1222→  if (filter?.format) {
  1223→    results = results.filter(m => m.format === filter.format);
  1224→  }
  1225→
  1226→  // Sort by date descending
  1227→  results.sort((a, b) => b.createdAt.getTime() - a.createdAt.getTime());
  1228→
  1229→  if (filter?.limit) {
  1230→    results = results.slice(0, filter.limit);
  1231→  }
  1232→
  1233→  return results;
  1234→}
  1235→
  1236→export async function getMaterialFile(id: string): Promise<Blob | undefined> {
  1237→  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  1238→  const file = await db.get('files', id);
  1239→  return file?.blob;
  1240→}
  1241→
  1242→export async function deleteMaterial(id: string): Promise<void> {
  1243→  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  1244→  await db.delete('files', id);
  1245→  await db.delete('metadata', id);
  1246→}
  1247→
  1248→async function generateThumbnail(blob: Blob, maxSize = 200): Promise<Blob> {
  1249→  return new Promise((resolve) => {
  1250→    const img = new Image();
  1251→    img.onload = () => {
  1252→      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
  1253→      const scale = Math.min(maxSize / img.width, maxSize / img.height);
  1254→      canvas.width = img.width * scale;
  1255→      canvas.height = img.height * scale;
  1256→
  1257→      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d')!;
  1258→      ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
  1259→
  1260→      canvas.toBlob((thumbBlob) => {
  1261→        resolve(thumbBlob || blob);
  1262→      }, 'image/jpeg', 0.7);
  1263→    };
  1264→    img.src = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
  1265→  });
  1266→}
  1267→```
  1268→

  1291→import type { ToolState } from '@/types/tools';
  1292→
  1293→interface ToolPanelProps {
  1294→  tool: ToolState | null;
  1295→  maestro: { name: string; avatar: string; color: string } | null;
  1296→  onClose: () => void;
  1297→  isMinimized?: boolean;
  1298→  onToggleMinimize?: () => void;
  1299→}
  1300→
  1301→export function ToolPanel({
  1302→  tool,
  1303→  maestro,
  1304→  onClose,
  1305→  isMinimized = false,
  1306→  onToggleMinimize,
  1307→}: ToolPanelProps) {
  1308→  if (!tool) return null;
  1309→
  1310→  const renderToolContent = () => {
  1311→    switch (tool.type) {
  1312→      case 'mindmap':
  1313→        return <MarkmapRenderer data={tool.content as { markdown: string }} />;
  1314→      case 'quiz':
  1315→        return <QuizTool data={tool.content} />;
  1316→      case 'demo':
  1317→        return <DemoSandbox data={tool.content as { html: string; css?: string; js?: string }} />;
  1318→      case 'search':
  1319→        return <SearchResults data={tool.content} />;
  1320→      case 'flashcard':
  1321→        return <FlashcardTool data={tool.content} />;
  1322→      default:
  1323→        return <div>Tool type not supported: {tool.type}</div>;
  1324→    }
  1325→  };
  1326→
  1327→  return (
  1328→    <AnimatePresence>
  1329→      <motion.div
  1330→        initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
  1331→        animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
  1332→        exit={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
  1333→        className={cn(
  1334→          'bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 rounded-xl shadow-xl border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700',
  1335→          'flex flex-col overflow-hidden',
  1336→          isMinimized ? 'h-16' : 'h-[70vh]'
  1337→        )}
  1338→      >
  1339→        {/* Header - 10% */}
  1340→        <div
  1341→          className="flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 border-b border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700"
  1342→          style={{ backgroundColor: maestro?.color ? `${maestro.color}10` : undefined }}
  1343→        >
  1344→          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
  1345→            {maestro && (
  1346→              <img
  1347→                src={maestro.avatar}
  1348→                alt={maestro.name}
  1349→                className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full"
  1350→              />
  1351→            )}
  1352→            <span className="font-medium">{maestro?.name || 'Tool'}</span>
  1353→            <span className="text-sm text-slate-500">
  1354→              {tool.status === 'building' ? 'Creazione in corso...' : tool.type}
  1355→            </span>
  1356→          </div>
  1357→
  1358→          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1359→            {onToggleMinimize && (
  1360→              <Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={onToggleMinimize}>
  1361→                {isMinimized ? <Maximize2 className="w-4 h-4" /> : <Minimize2 className="w-4 h-4" />}
  1362→              </Button>
  1363→            )}
  1364→            <Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={onClose}>
  1365→              <X className="w-4 h-4" />
  1366→            </Button>
  1367→          </div>
  1368→        </div>
  1369→
  1370→        {/* Content - 70% */}
  1371→        {!isMinimized && (
  1372→          <div className="flex-1 overflow-auto p-4">
  1373→            {tool.status === 'building' ? (
  1374→              <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full">
  1375→                <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-12 w-12 border-b-2 border-primary" />
  1376→                <p className="mt-4 text-slate-500">
  1377→                  {Math.round(tool.progress * 100)}% completato...
  1378→                </p>
  1379→              </div>
  1380→            ) : tool.status === 'error' ? (
  1381→              <div className="text-red-500 text-center">
  1382→                Errore nella creazione del tool
  1383→              </div>
  1384→            ) : (
  1385→              renderToolContent()
  1386→            )}
  1387→          </div>
  1388→        )}
  1389→      </motion.div>
  1390→    </AnimatePresence>
  1391→  );
  1392→}
  1393→```

  1451→### T-09: Demo Sandbox (#23)
  1452→
  1453→Create `src/components/tools/demo-sandbox.tsx`:
  1454→
  1455→```typescript
  1456→'use client';
  1457→
  1458→import { useState, useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
  1459→import { RefreshCw, AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react';
  1460→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
  1461→
  1462→interface DemoSandboxProps {
  1463→  data: {
  1464→    title?: string;
  1465→    html: string;
  1466→    css?: string;
  1467→    js?: string;
  1468→  };
  1469→}
  1470→
  1471→// Dangerous patterns to block
  1472→const DANGEROUS_PATTERNS = [
  1473→  /document\.cookie/i,
  1474→  /localStorage/i,
  1475→  /sessionStorage/i,
  1476→  /fetch\s*\(/i,
  1477→  /XMLHttpRequest/i,
  1478→  /window\.open/i,
  1479→  /window\.location/i,
  1480→  /eval\s*\(/i,
  1481→  /Function\s*\(/i,
  1482→];
  1483→
  1484→function validateCode(code: string): { safe: boolean; warnings: string[] } {
  1485→  const warnings: string[] = [];
  1486→
  1487→  for (const pattern of DANGEROUS_PATTERNS) {
  1488→    if (pattern.test(code)) {
  1489→      warnings.push(`Blocked: ${pattern.source}`);
  1490→    }
  1491→  }
  1492→
  1493→  return { safe: warnings.length === 0, warnings };
  1494→}
  1495→
  1496→export function DemoSandbox({ data }: DemoSandboxProps) {
  1497→  const iframeRef = useRef<HTMLIFrameElement>(null);
  1498→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  1499→  const [key, setKey] = useState(0);
  1500→
  1501→  const { html, css = '', js = '' } = data;
  1502→
  1503→  // Validate JS code
  1504→  const validation = validateCode(js);
  1505→
  1506→  const fullHtml = `
  1507→    <!DOCTYPE html>
  1508→    <html>
  1509→    <head>
  1510→      <meta charset="UTF-8">
  1511→      <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; script-src 'unsafe-inline';">
  1512→      <style>
  1513→        body { margin: 0; padding: 16px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
  1514→        ${css}
  1515→      </style>
  1516→    </head>
  1517→    <body>
  1518→      ${html}
  1519→      ${validation.safe ? `<script>${js}</script>` : '<!-- JS blocked for safety -->'}
  1520→    </body>
  1521→    </html>
  1522→  `;
  1523→
  1524→  const handleRefresh = () => {
  1525→    setKey(prev => prev + 1);
  1526→    setError(null);
  1527→  };
  1528→
  1529→  if (!validation.safe) {
  1530→    return (
  1531→      <div className="p-4 bg-yellow-50 dark:bg-yellow-900/20 rounded-lg">
  1532→        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-yellow-700 dark:text-yellow-400 mb-2">
  1533→          <AlertTriangle className="w-5 h-5" />
  1534→          <span className="font-medium">Demo bloccata per sicurezza</span>
  1535→        </div>
  1536→        <ul className="text-sm text-yellow-600 dark:text-yellow-500 list-disc list-inside">
  1537→          {validation.warnings.map((w, i) => (
  1538→            <li key={i}>{w}</li>
  1539→          ))}
  1540→        </ul>
  1541→      </div>
  1542→    );
  1543→  }
  1544→
  1545→  return (
  1546→    <div className="flex flex-col h-full">
  1547→      <div className="flex justify-between items-center mb-2">
  1548→        <h3 className="font-medium">{data.title || 'Simulazione Interattiva'}</h3>
  1549→        <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={handleRefresh}>
  1550→          <RefreshCw className="w-4 h-4 mr-1" />
  1551→          Ricarica
  1552→        </Button>
  1553→      </div>
  1554→
  1555→      <div className="flex-1 border rounded-lg overflow-hidden bg-white">
  1556→        <iframe
  1557→          key={key}
  1558→          ref={iframeRef}
  1559→          srcDoc={fullHtml}
  1560→          sandbox="allow-scripts"
  1561→          className="w-full h-full border-0"
  1562→          title="Demo interattiva"
  1563→        />
  1564→      </div>
  1565→    </div>
  1566→  );
  1567→}
  1568→```

  1684→// Call after session ends in conversation-flow.tsx
  1685→export function onSessionComplete(params: {

  1702→}
  1703→
  1704→// Call when achievement unlocked

  1707→}
  1708→
  1709→// Call when streak reaches milestones

  1715→

  1720→
  1721→// Call when flashcards are due for review
  1722→export function onFlashcardsDue(count: number) {
  1723→  notificationService.flashcardReview(count);

  1891→### T-16: Unified Archive Page (#37)
  1892→
  1893→Create `src/components/education/archive-view.tsx`:
  1894→
  1895→```typescript
  1896→'use client';
  1897→
  1898→import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
  1899→import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent } from '@/components/ui/tabs';
  1900→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
  1901→import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select';
  1902→import { Grid, List, Search, Star, Calendar, Eye } from 'lucide-react';
  1903→import { getMaterials } from '@/lib/storage/materials-db';
  1904→import type { ToolType } from '@/types/tools';
  1905→
  1906→type FilterType = 'all' | ToolType;
  1907→type SortBy = 'date' | 'rating' | 'views';
  1908→
  1909→export function ArchiveView() {
  1910→  const [filter, setFilter] = useState<FilterType>('all');
  1911→  const [sortBy, setSortBy] = useState<SortBy>('date');
  1912→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('');
  1913→  const [viewMode, setViewMode] = useState<'grid' | 'list'>('grid');
  1914→  const [tools, setTools] = useState([]);
  1915→  const [materials, setMaterials] = useState([]);
  1916→
  1917→  useEffect(() => {
  1918→    // Fetch tools from API
  1919→    fetch('/api/tools/saved')
  1920→      .then(r => r.json())
  1921→      .then(data => setTools(data.tools || []));
  1922→
  1923→    // Fetch materials from IndexedDB
  1924→    getMaterials().then(setMaterials);
  1925→  }, []);
  1926→
  1927→  // Combine and filter results
  1928→  const allItems = [
  1929→    ...tools.map(t => ({ ...t, source: 'tool' })),
  1930→    ...materials.map(m => ({ ...m, source: 'material', type: m.format })),
  1931→  ];
  1932→
  1933→  const filtered = allItems
  1934→    .filter(item => filter === 'all' || item.type === filter)
  1935→    .filter(item =>
  1936→      !searchQuery ||
  1937→      item.title?.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()) ||
  1938→      item.topic?.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())
  1939→    )
  1940→    .sort((a, b) => {
  1941→      if (sortBy === 'date') return new Date(b.createdAt).getTime() - new Date(a.createdAt).getTime();
  1942→      if (sortBy === 'rating') return (b.userRating || 0) - (a.userRating || 0);
  1943→      if (sortBy === 'views') return (b.viewCount || 0) - (a.viewCount || 0);
  1944→      return 0;
  1945→    });
  1946→
  1947→  return (
  1948→    <div className="space-y-6">
  1949→      {/* Header */}
  1950→      <div className="flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-4 items-start sm:items-center justify-between">
  1951→        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold">Archivio</h1>
  1952→
  1953→        <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1954→          {/* Search */}
  1955→          <div className="relative">
  1956→            <Search className="absolute left-2 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-slate-400" />
  1957→            <Input
  1958→              placeholder="Cerca..."
  1959→              value={searchQuery}
  1960→              onChange={e => setSearchQuery(e.target.value)}
  1961→              className="pl-8 w-48"
  1962→            />
  1963→          </div>
  1964→
  1965→          {/* Sort */}
  1966→          <Select value={sortBy} onValueChange={(v) => setSortBy(v as SortBy)}>
  1967→            <SelectTrigger className="w-32">
  1968→              <SelectValue />
  1969→            </SelectTrigger>
  1970→            <SelectContent>
  1971→              <SelectItem value="date"><Calendar className="w-4 h-4 inline mr-1" />Data</SelectItem>
  1972→              <SelectItem value="rating"><Star className="w-4 h-4 inline mr-1" />Voto</SelectItem>
  1973→              <SelectItem value="views"><Eye className="w-4 h-4 inline mr-1" />Visite</SelectItem>
  1974→            </SelectContent>
  1975→          </Select>
  1976→
  1977→          {/* View toggle */}
  1978→          <div className="flex border rounded-lg">
  1979→            <button
  1980→              onClick={() => setViewMode('grid')}
  1981→              className={cn('p-2', viewMode === 'grid' && 'bg-slate-100 dark:bg-slate-800')}
  1982→            >
  1983→              <Grid className="w-4 h-4" />
  1984→            </button>
  1985→            <button
  1986→              onClick={() => setViewMode('list')}
  1987→              className={cn('p-2', viewMode === 'list' && 'bg-slate-100 dark:bg-slate-800')}
  1988→            >
  1989→              <List className="w-4 h-4" />
  1990→            </button>
  1991→          </div>
  1992→        </div>
  1993→      </div>
  1994→
  1995→      {/* Tabs */}
  1996→      <Tabs value={filter} onValueChange={(v) => setFilter(v as FilterType)}>
  1997→        <TabsList>
  1998→          <TabsTrigger value="all">Tutti ({allItems.length})</TabsTrigger>
  1999→          <TabsTrigger value="mindmap">Mappe</TabsTrigger>
  2000→          <TabsTrigger value="quiz">Quiz</TabsTrigger>
  2001→          <TabsTrigger value="flashcard">Flashcard</TabsTrigger>
  2002→          <TabsTrigger value="demo">Demo</TabsTrigger>
  2003→          <TabsTrigger value="image">Foto</TabsTrigger>
  2004→          <TabsTrigger value="pdf">PDF</TabsTrigger>
  2005→        </TabsList>
  2006→
  2007→        <TabsContent value={filter} className="mt-4">
  2008→          {filtered.length === 0 ? (
  2009→            <EmptyState filter={filter} />
  2010→          ) : viewMode === 'grid' ? (
  2011→            <GridView items={filtered} />
  2012→          ) : (
  2013→            <ListView items={filtered} />
  2014→          )}
  2015→        </TabsContent>
  2016→      </Tabs>
  2017→    </div>
  2018→  );
  2019→}
  2020→```

  2046→      variant="ghost"

  2058→    >

  2060→    </Button>
  2061→    <Button
  2062→      variant="ghost"
  2063→      size="sm"
  2064→      className="h-7 px-2 text-xs"
  2065→      onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onToolRequest(maestro, 'demo'); }}
  2066→    >
  2067→      <Play className="w-3 h-3 mr-1" />Demo
  2068→    </Button>
  2069→    <Button
  2070→      variant="ghost"
  2071→      size="sm"
  2072→      className="h-7 px-2 text-xs"
  2073→      onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onToolRequest(maestro, 'webcam'); }}
  2074→    >

  2083→### T-18: Persist Tools to Database (#37)
  2084→
  2085→Create `src/lib/tools/tool-persistence.ts`:
  2086→
  2087→```typescript
  2088→import { prisma } from '@/lib/db';
  2089→import type { ToolType } from '@/types/tools';
  2090→
  2091→export async function saveTool(params: {
  2092→  userId: string;
  2093→  type: ToolType;
  2094→  title: string;
  2095→  topic?: string;
  2096→  content: unknown;
  2097→  maestroId?: string;
  2098→  conversationId?: string;
  2099→  sessionId?: string;
  2100→}) {
  2101→  return prisma.createdTool.create({
  2102→    data: {
  2103→      ...params,
  2104→      content: JSON.stringify(params.content),
  2105→    },
  2106→  });
  2107→}
  2108→
  2109→export async function getUserTools(
  2110→  userId: string,
  2111→  filter?: {
  2112→    type?: ToolType;
  2113→    isBookmarked?: boolean;
  2114→    limit?: number;
  2115→    offset?: number;
  2116→  }
  2117→) {
  2118→  return prisma.createdTool.findMany({
  2119→    where: {
  2120→      userId,
  2121→      ...(filter?.type && { type: filter.type }),
  2122→      ...(filter?.isBookmarked && { isBookmarked: true }),
  2123→    },
  2124→    orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
  2125→    take: filter?.limit || 50,
  2126→    skip: filter?.offset || 0,
  2127→  });
  2128→}
  2129→
  2130→export async function deleteTool(id: string, userId: string) {
  2131→  return prisma.createdTool.deleteMany({
  2132→    where: { id, userId },
  2133→  });
  2134→}
  2135→
  2136→export async function updateToolRating(id: string, userId: string, rating: number) {
  2137→  return prisma.createdTool.updateMany({
  2138→    where: { id, userId },
  2139→    data: { userRating: rating },
  2140→  });
  2141→}
  2142→
  2143→export async function toggleBookmark(id: string, userId: string) {
  2144→  const tool = await prisma.createdTool.findFirst({ where: { id, userId } });
  2145→  if (!tool) return null;
  2146→
  2147→  return prisma.createdTool.update({
  2148→    where: { id },
  2149→    data: { isBookmarked: !tool.isBookmarked },
  2150→  });
  2151→}
  2152→
  2153→export async function incrementViewCount(id: string) {
  2154→  return prisma.createdTool.update({
  2155→    where: { id },
  2156→    data: { viewCount: { increment: 1 } },
  2157→  });
  2158→}
  2159→```
  2160→
  2161→Create API route `src/app/api/tools/saved/route.ts`:
  2162→
  2163→```typescript
  2164→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
  2165→import { getUserTools, saveTool, deleteTool, updateToolRating, toggleBookmark } from '@/lib/tools/tool-persistence';
  2166→
  2167→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
  2168→  const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);
  2169→  const userId = searchParams.get('userId');
  2170→  const type = searchParams.get('type');
  2171→  const bookmarked = searchParams.get('bookmarked') === 'true';
  2172→
  2173→  if (!userId) {
  2174→    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'userId required' }, { status: 400 });
  2175→  }
  2176→
  2177→  const tools = await getUserTools(userId, {
  2178→    type: type as ToolType | undefined,
  2179→    isBookmarked: bookmarked || undefined,
  2180→  });
  2181→
  2182→  return NextResponse.json({ tools });
  2183→}
  2184→
  2185→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
  2186→  const body = await request.json();
  2187→  const tool = await saveTool(body);
  2188→  return NextResponse.json({ tool });
  2189→}
  2190→
  2191→export async function DELETE(request: NextRequest) {
  2192→  const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);
  2193→  const id = searchParams.get('id');
  2194→  const userId = searchParams.get('userId');
  2195→
  2196→  if (!id || !userId) {
  2197→    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'id and userId required' }, { status: 400 });
  2198→  }
  2199→
  2200→  await deleteTool(id, userId);
  2201→  return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
  2202→}
  2203→```

# MaestroToolsPlanDec30 - Interactive Tools & Materials System

**Data**: 2025-12-30
**Target**: Complete tool execution + Materials archive + UI improvements
**Metodo**: VERIFICA BRUTALE - ogni task testato prima di dichiararlo fatto

---

## 🎯 ESEGUI PIANO - UN SOLO COMANDO

**Apri Kitty, poi copia e incolla QUESTO:**

```
Esegui il piano in /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md - Sei il COORDINATORE (CLAUDE 1). Workflow: 1) Crea branch e worktree per ogni Claude (vedi sezione GIT WORKFLOW), 2) Lancia 4 Claude con claude-parallel.sh, 3) Invia ogni Claude al suo worktree, 4) Invia i task, 5) Monitora e sincronizza i gate, 6) Quando ogni Claude finisce deve fare commit+push+PR, 7) Tu fai merge delle PR in ordine, 8) Cleanup worktree e branch. Segui le regole BRUTAL MODE. Mai chiudere issue senza prova.
```

**Tutto qui. CLAUDE 1 leggerà il piano e farà il resto.**

---

## 🚀 KITTY ORCHESTRATION - DETTAGLI

> **LEGGI PRIMA DI TUTTO** - Istruzioni per parallelizzare

### Pre-requisiti
```bash
# DEVE essere eseguito da Kitty (non Warp, non iTerm)
# Verifica di essere in Kitty:
echo $KITTY_PID  # Deve stampare un numero

# Verifica remote control attivo:
~/.claude/scripts/kitty-check.sh
```

### GIT WORKFLOW (OBBLIGATORIO)

**Ogni Claude lavora in un worktree separato. Ogni fase = 1 PR.**

#### STEP 0: Setup Worktrees (CLAUDE 1 fa questo PRIMA di tutto)

```bash
cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu

# Crea branch per ogni fase
git checkout MirrorBuddy
git branch feature/tools-phase1-backend
git branch feature/tools-phase2-ui
git branch feature/tools-phase3-integration

# Crea worktree per ogni Claude
git worktree add ../ConvergioEdu-C2 feature/tools-phase1-backend
git worktree add ../ConvergioEdu-C3 feature/tools-phase2-ui
git worktree add ../ConvergioEdu-C4 feature/tools-phase3-integration

# Verifica
git worktree list
```

#### Mapping Claude → Worktree → Branch

| Claude | Worktree | Branch | PR |
|--------|----------|--------|-----|
| CLAUDE 1 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu` | MirrorBuddy | Coordina solo |
| CLAUDE 2 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C2` | feature/tools-phase1-backend | PR #1 |
| CLAUDE 3 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C3` | feature/tools-phase2-ui | PR #2 |
| CLAUDE 4 | `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C4` | feature/tools-phase3-integration | PR #3 |

---

### Launch Sequence (DA ESEGUIRE IN ORDINE)

**STEP 1: Lancia i Claude worker (dal terminale Kitty)**
```bash
~/.claude/scripts/claude-parallel.sh 4
```

**STEP 2: Invia ogni Claude al suo worktree**
```bash
kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-2 "cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C2" && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-2 Return
kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-3 "cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C3" && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-3 Return
kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-4 "cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu-C4" && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-4 Return
```

**STEP 3: Invia i task**
```bash
kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-2 "Leggi /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md, sei CLAUDE 2 (BACKEND). Lavori nel worktree feature/tools-phase1-backend. Esegui T-00 → T-06. Quando finisci: commit, push, crea PR verso MirrorBuddy." && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-2 Return

kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-3 "Leggi /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md, sei CLAUDE 3 (FRONTEND). Lavori nel worktree feature/tools-phase2-ui. ATTENDI GATE-1 poi esegui T-07 → T-12. Quando finisci: commit, push, crea PR." && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-3 Return

kitty @ send-text --match title:Claude-4 "Leggi /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/docs/plans/MaestroToolsPlanDec30.md, sei CLAUDE 4 (INTEGRATION). Lavori nel worktree feature/tools-phase3-integration. ATTENDI GATE-2 poi esegui T-15 → T-22. Quando finisci: commit, push, crea PR." && kitty @ send-key --match title:Claude-4 Return
```

---

### PR Workflow (ogni Claude fa questo quando completa la sua fase)

```bash
# 1. Commit tutto
git add .
git commit -m "feat(tools): Phase X - [description]

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"

# 2. Push
git push -u origin feature/tools-phaseX-[name]

# 3. Crea PR
gh pr create --title "feat(tools): Phase X - [description]" --body "## Summary
- [bullet points]

## Issues Closed
- Closes #XX
- Closes #YY

## Test Plan
- [ ] npm run lint ✅
- [ ] npm run typecheck ✅
- [ ] npm run build ✅
- [ ] Manual test: [description]

🤖 Generated with Claude Code" --base MirrorBuddy
```

---

### Merge & Cleanup (CLAUDE 1 fa questo alla fine)

```bash
cd /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu

# 1. Merge tutte le PR (in ordine!)
gh pr merge [PR-1-number] --merge
gh pr merge [PR-2-number] --merge
gh pr merge [PR-3-number] --merge

# 2. Pull changes
git pull origin MirrorBuddy

# 3. Cleanup worktrees
git worktree remove ../ConvergioEdu-C2
git worktree remove ../ConvergioEdu-C3
git worktree remove ../ConvergioEdu-C4

# 4. Cleanup branches
git branch -d feature/tools-phase1-backend
git branch -d feature/tools-phase2-ui

## OVERVIEW

### The Problem
1. **#39**: Chat API has NO function calling - Maestros just output text
2. **#36**: No Tool Panel - tools render inline in chat (unusable)
3. **#35, #38**: No tool buttons on maestro cards or conversation
4. **#37**: No Archive - all created content is LOST
5. **#22**: No IndexedDB storage for materials
6. **#19**: Still says "Compiti", no Subject→Maestro flow
7. **#14, #27**: Notifications exist but NEVER triggered

### The Solution
Complete system with:
- OpenAI function calling in chat API (#39)
- Tool panel with video conference layout (#36)
- Tool buttons everywhere (#35, #38)
- Unified archive for ALL content (#37)
- IndexedDB for materials (#22)
- Materials page redesign (#19)
- Notification triggers (#14)

---

## USE CASES

| Action | Tool | Result |
|--------|------|--------|
| Click "Mappa" | Mind Map | Maestro creates interactive mind map |
| Click "Quiz" | Quiz | Maestro creates quiz, evaluates answers |
| Click "Demo" | Demo | Maestro generates HTML/JS simulation |
| Click "Cerca" | Search | Search web/YouTube for educational content |
| Click "Flashcard" | Flashcard | Maestro creates flashcard deck |
| Click "Webcam" | Upload | Capture photo → detect subject → route to Maestro |
| Upload PDF | Upload | PDF pages → detect subject → route to Maestro |
| Browse Archive | Archive | Filter/sort all created tools and materials |

## ARCHITECTURE

### Tool Execution Flow
```
Button click OR Maestro decides
        ↓
Chat API with OpenAI function calling (#39)
        ↓
Tool handler executes
        ↓
SSE broadcasts updates (#26)
        ↓
Tool Panel renders in real-time (#36)
        ↓
Save to Archive (IndexedDB #22 + Prisma)
        ↓
Available in Archive page (#37)
```

### Video Conference Layout (#36)
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Avatar] Maestro Name    [Close Panel]  │  10%
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤

├─────────────────────────────────────────┤

```

```

        ↓

        ↓
Subject detected (math, science, etc.)
        ↓
User confirms subject
        ↓
Route to appropriate Maestro
        ↓
Maieutic guidance begins
        ↓
Save to Materials Archive (#37)
```

## CLAUDE 2: PHASE 0 (Preparation)

### T-00: ADR - Tool Execution Architecture

Create `docs/adr/0004-tool-execution-architecture.md`:

```markdown
# ADR 0004: Tool Execution Architecture

## Status
Accepted

## Context
I Maestri AI devono poter creare strumenti interattivi (mind maps, quiz, demo, flashcard)
durante le conversazioni. Attualmente il chat API NON ha function calling - i Maestri
scrivono solo testo come "Usa lo strumento create_mindmap..." senza eseguire nulla.

Problemi attuali:
1. Chat API non supporta OpenAI function calling
2. Nessun tool panel per visualizzare i tool creati
3. Nessun storage per salvare i tool
4. Nessun archivio per rivedere tool passati

Opzioni considerate:
1. Client-side parsing del testo → fragile, non affidabile
2. Streaming custom → complesso, non standard
3. OpenAI function calling → standard, supportato, affidabile

## Decision
Implementiamo OpenAI function calling nel chat API con:
- `tools` parameter con CHAT_TOOL_DEFINITIONS
- Tool executor framework con handler registry
- SSE per aggiornamenti real-time
- IndexedDB per storage materiali
- Prisma CreatedTool model per persistenza

## Consequences

### Positive
- Standard OpenAI API, ben documentato
- Affidabile: il modello decide quando chiamare i tool
- Estensibile: nuovi tool = nuovi handler
- Real-time: SSE per progress updates

### Negative
- Richiede Azure OpenAI (Ollama non supporta function calling)
- Complessità aggiuntiva nel frontend

## References
- OpenAI Function Calling: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling
- Issue #39: Chat API missing function calling
- Issue #23: Conversation-First Architecture
```

### T-00d: Update CLAUDE.md with Tool Section

```markdown
### Tool Execution System

> **Architecture Decision**: ADR 0004 - Tool Execution Architecture

The Maestri can create interactive tools during conversations using OpenAI function calling.

#### Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/types/tools.ts` | Tool types and OpenAI function definitions |
| `src/lib/tools/tool-executor.ts` | Handler registry and execution |
| `src/lib/tools/handlers/` | Individual tool handlers |
| `src/components/tools/tool-panel.tsx` | Tool visualization panel |
| `src/components/tools/demo-sandbox.tsx` | Secure iframe for demos |
| `src/lib/storage/materials-db.ts` | IndexedDB for materials |

#### Tool Types

\`\`\`typescript
type ToolType = 'mindmap' | 'quiz' | 'flashcard' | 'demo' | 'search' | 'webcam' | 'pdf';
\`\`\`

#### Tool Flow

\`\`\`
User clicks tool button OR Maestro decides
        ↓
Chat API with OpenAI function calling
        ↓
Tool executor routes to handler
        ↓
Handler generates content
        ↓
Tool panel renders result
        ↓
Saved to Archive (IndexedDB + Prisma)
\`\`\`

#### Adding a New Tool

1. Add type to `ToolType` in `src/types/tools.ts`
2. Add function definition to `CHAT_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`
3. Create handler in `src/lib/tools/handlers/[tool]-handler.ts`
4. Register handler in `src/lib/tools/handlers/index.ts`
5. Add rendering case to `ToolPanel` component
6. Write tests in `__tests__/[tool]-handler.test.ts`
```

          cards: {
            type: 'array',
            items: {
              type: 'object',
              properties: {
                front: { type: 'string' },
                back: { type: 'string' },
              },
            },
          },
        },
        required: ['topic', 'cards'],
      },
    },
  },
];

// Tool state for real-time updates
export interface ToolState {
  id: string;
  type: ToolType;
  status: 'initializing' | 'building' | 'completed' | 'error';
  progress: number;
  content: unknown;
  createdAt: Date;
}

// Execution result
export interface ToolExecutionResult {
  success: boolean;
  toolId: string;
  toolType: ToolType;
  data?: unknown;
  error?: string;
}

### T-03: Tool Executor Framework (#23)

Create `src/lib/tools/tool-executor.ts`:

```typescript
import { ToolType, ToolExecutionResult } from '@/types/tools';
import { nanoid } from 'nanoid';
import { broadcastToolEvent } from '@/lib/realtime/tool-events';

type ToolHandler = (
  args: Record<string, unknown>,
  context: { sessionId?: string; userId?: string; maestroId?: string }
) => Promise<ToolExecutionResult>;

const handlers = new Map<string, ToolHandler>();

export function registerToolHandler(functionName: string, handler: ToolHandler) {
  handlers.set(functionName, handler);
}

export async function executeToolCall(
  functionName: string,
  args: Record<string, unknown>,
  context: { sessionId?: string; userId?: string; maestroId?: string }
): Promise<ToolExecutionResult> {
  const handler = handlers.get(functionName);

  if (!handler) {
    return {
      success: false,
      toolId: nanoid(),
      toolType: 'mindmap',
      error: `Unknown tool: ${functionName}`,
    };
  }

  const toolId = nanoid();

  // Broadcast start event
  broadcastToolEvent({
    type: 'tool_started',
    toolId,
    functionName,
    timestamp: new Date(),
  });

  try {
    const result = await handler(args, context);

    // Broadcast complete event
    broadcastToolEvent({
      type: 'tool_completed',
      toolId,
      data: result.data,
      timestamp: new Date(),
    });

    return result;
  } catch (error) {
    broadcastToolEvent({
      type: 'tool_error',
      toolId,
      error: String(error),
      timestamp: new Date(),
    });

    return {
      success: false,
      toolId,
      toolType: 'mindmap',
      error: String(error),
    };
  }
}
```

### T-06: IndexedDB Storage for Materials (#22)

Create `src/lib/storage/materials-db.ts`:

```typescript
import { openDB, DBSchema, IDBPDatabase } from 'idb';

interface MaterialsDB extends DBSchema {
  files: {
    key: string;
    value: {
      id: string;
      blob: Blob;
      thumbnail?: Blob;
      createdAt: number;
    };
  };
  metadata: {
    key: string;
    value: {
      id: string;
      filename: string;
      format: 'image' | 'pdf';
      mimeType: string;
      subject?: string;
      maestroId?: string;
      size: number;
      pageCount?: number;
      createdAt: Date;
      updatedAt: Date;
    };
    indexes: {
      'by-date': Date;
      'by-subject': string;
      'by-format': string;
    };
  };
}

let dbInstance: IDBPDatabase<MaterialsDB> | null = null;

export async function getMaterialsDB(): Promise<IDBPDatabase<MaterialsDB>> {
  if (dbInstance) return dbInstance;

  dbInstance = await openDB<MaterialsDB>('convergio-materials', 1, {
    upgrade(db) {
      // Files store (blobs)
      db.createObjectStore('files', { keyPath: 'id' });

      // Metadata store
      const metaStore = db.createObjectStore('metadata', { keyPath: 'id' });
      metaStore.createIndex('by-date', 'createdAt');
      metaStore.createIndex('by-subject', 'subject');
      metaStore.createIndex('by-format', 'format');
    },
  });

  return dbInstance;
}

export async function saveMaterial(
  file: Blob,
  metadata: Omit<MaterialsDB['metadata']['value'], 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>
): Promise<string> {
  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  const id = crypto.randomUUID();
  const now = new Date();

  // Generate thumbnail for images
  let thumbnail: Blob | undefined;
  if (metadata.format === 'image') {
    thumbnail = await generateThumbnail(file);
  }

  await db.put('files', {
    id,
    blob: file,
    thumbnail,
    createdAt: now.getTime(),
  });

  await db.put('metadata', {
    ...metadata,
    id,
    createdAt: now,
    updatedAt: now,
  });

  return id;
}

export async function getMaterials(filter?: {
  subject?: string;
  format?: 'image' | 'pdf';
  limit?: number;
}): Promise<Array<MaterialsDB['metadata']['value']>> {
  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  let results = await db.getAll('metadata');

  if (filter?.subject) {
    results = results.filter(m => m.subject === filter.subject);
  }
  if (filter?.format) {
    results = results.filter(m => m.format === filter.format);
  }

  // Sort by date descending
  results.sort((a, b) => b.createdAt.getTime() - a.createdAt.getTime());

  if (filter?.limit) {
    results = results.slice(0, filter.limit);
  }

  return results;
}

export async function getMaterialFile(id: string): Promise<Blob | undefined> {
  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  const file = await db.get('files', id);
  return file?.blob;
}

export async function deleteMaterial(id: string): Promise<void> {
  const db = await getMaterialsDB();
  await db.delete('files', id);
  await db.delete('metadata', id);
}

async function generateThumbnail(blob: Blob, maxSize = 200): Promise<Blob> {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const img = new Image();
    img.onload = () => {
      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
      const scale = Math.min(maxSize / img.width, maxSize / img.height);
      canvas.width = img.width * scale;
      canvas.height = img.height * scale;

      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d')!;
      ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);

      canvas.toBlob((thumbBlob) => {
        resolve(thumbBlob || blob);
      }, 'image/jpeg', 0.7);
    };
    img.src = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
  });
}
```

Add dependency: `npm install idb`

'use client';

import { useState } from 'react';
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';
import { X, Minimize2, Maximize2 } from 'lucide-react';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
import { MarkmapRenderer } from './markmap-renderer';
import { QuizTool } from './quiz-tool';
import { DemoSandbox } from './demo-sandbox';
import { SearchResults } from './search-results';
import { FlashcardTool } from './flashcard-tool';
import type { ToolState } from '@/types/tools';

interface ToolPanelProps {
  tool: ToolState | null;
  maestro: { name: string; avatar: string; color: string } | null;
  onClose: () => void;
  isMinimized?: boolean;
  onToggleMinimize?: () => void;
}

export function ToolPanel({
  tool,
  maestro,
  onClose,
  isMinimized = false,
  onToggleMinimize,
}: ToolPanelProps) {
  if (!tool) return null;

  const renderToolContent = () => {
    switch (tool.type) {
      case 'mindmap':
        return <MarkmapRenderer data={tool.content as { markdown: string }} />;
      case 'quiz':
        return <QuizTool data={tool.content} />;
      case 'demo':
        return <DemoSandbox data={tool.content as { html: string; css?: string; js?: string }} />;
      case 'search':
        return <SearchResults data={tool.content} />;
      case 'flashcard':
        return <FlashcardTool data={tool.content} />;
      default:
        return <div>Tool type not supported: {tool.type}</div>;
    }
  };

  return (
    <AnimatePresence>
      <motion.div
        initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
        animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
        exit={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
        className={cn(
          'bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 rounded-xl shadow-xl border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700',
          'flex flex-col overflow-hidden',
          isMinimized ? 'h-16' : 'h-[70vh]'
        )}
      >
        {/* Header - 10% */}
        <div
          className="flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 border-b border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700"
          style={{ backgroundColor: maestro?.color ? `${maestro.color}10` : undefined }}
        >
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            {maestro && (
              <img
                src={maestro.avatar}
                alt={maestro.name}
                className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full"
              />
            )}
            <span className="font-medium">{maestro?.name || 'Tool'}</span>
            <span className="text-sm text-slate-500">
              {tool.status === 'building' ? 'Creazione in corso...' : tool.type}
            </span>
          </div>

          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
            {onToggleMinimize && (
              <Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={onToggleMinimize}>
                {isMinimized ? <Maximize2 className="w-4 h-4" /> : <Minimize2 className="w-4 h-4" />}
              </Button>
            )}
            <Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={onClose}>
              <X className="w-4 h-4" />
            </Button>
          </div>
        </div>

        {/* Content - 70% */}
        {!isMinimized && (
          <div className="flex-1 overflow-auto p-4">
            {tool.status === 'building' ? (
              <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full">
                <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-12 w-12 border-b-2 border-primary" />
                <p className="mt-4 text-slate-500">
                  {Math.round(tool.progress * 100)}% completato...
                </p>
              </div>
            ) : tool.status === 'error' ? (
              <div className="text-red-500 text-center">
                Errore nella creazione del tool
              </div>
            ) : (
              renderToolContent()
            )}
          </div>
        )}
      </motion.div>
    </AnimatePresence>
  );
}
```

### T-09: Demo Sandbox (#23)

Create `src/components/tools/demo-sandbox.tsx`:

```typescript
'use client';

import { useState, useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
import { RefreshCw, AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';

interface DemoSandboxProps {
  data: {
    title?: string;
    html: string;
    css?: string;
    js?: string;
  };
}

// Dangerous patterns to block
const DANGEROUS_PATTERNS = [
  /document\.cookie/i,
  /localStorage/i,
  /sessionStorage/i,
  /fetch\s*\(/i,
  /XMLHttpRequest/i,
  /window\.open/i,
  /window\.location/i,
  /eval\s*\(/i,
  /Function\s*\(/i,
];

function validateCode(code: string): { safe: boolean; warnings: string[] } {
  const warnings: string[] = [];

  for (const pattern of DANGEROUS_PATTERNS) {
    if (pattern.test(code)) {
      warnings.push(`Blocked: ${pattern.source}`);
    }
  }

  return { safe: warnings.length === 0, warnings };
}

export function DemoSandbox({ data }: DemoSandboxProps) {
  const iframeRef = useRef<HTMLIFrameElement>(null);
  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const [key, setKey] = useState(0);

  const { html, css = '', js = '' } = data;

  // Validate JS code
  const validation = validateCode(js);

  const fullHtml = `
    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html>
    <head>
      <meta charset="UTF-8">
      <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; script-src 'unsafe-inline';">
      <style>
        body { margin: 0; padding: 16px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
        ${css}
      </style>
    </head>
    <body>
      ${html}
      ${validation.safe ? `<script>${js}</script>` : '<!-- JS blocked for safety -->'}
    </body>
    </html>
  `;

  const handleRefresh = () => {
    setKey(prev => prev + 1);
    setError(null);
  };

  if (!validation.safe) {
    return (
      <div className="p-4 bg-yellow-50 dark:bg-yellow-900/20 rounded-lg">
        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-yellow-700 dark:text-yellow-400 mb-2">
          <AlertTriangle className="w-5 h-5" />
          <span className="font-medium">Demo bloccata per sicurezza</span>
        </div>
        <ul className="text-sm text-yellow-600 dark:text-yellow-500 list-disc list-inside">
          {validation.warnings.map((w, i) => (
            <li key={i}>{w}</li>
          ))}
        </ul>
      </div>
    );
  }

  return (
    <div className="flex flex-col h-full">
      <div className="flex justify-between items-center mb-2">
        <h3 className="font-medium">{data.title || 'Simulazione Interattiva'}</h3>
        <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={handleRefresh}>
          <RefreshCw className="w-4 h-4 mr-1" />
          Ricarica
        </Button>
      </div>

      <div className="flex-1 border rounded-lg overflow-hidden bg-white">
        <iframe
          key={key}
          ref={iframeRef}
          srcDoc={fullHtml}
          sandbox="allow-scripts"
          className="w-full h-full border-0"
          title="Demo interattiva"
        />
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```
